sect2.title: Introduction to CUDA
sect2.para: The CUDA proprietary toolkit provides tools for running code on NVIDIA GPUs. This is otherwise known as hardware acceleration.

important.para: The NVIDIA driver version listed in the runfile name is the minimum NVIDIA driver version required. You must install an older version of CUDA if you cannot install a newer driver version.

note.para: The download size is over 5G, so you should consider if and when you will want to download this runfile. For this reason, it is not recommended to download it when using limited hotspot data. Its size can largely be attributed to containing compilers as well as the NVIDIA driver. The driver only accounts for 10% of the runfile's size.

sect2.title: Installation of CUDA
sect2.para: First create the installation directory and symlink as the &root; user:
Install CUDA by running the following commands as the &root; user:

screen.userinput: sh cuda_&cuda-version;_&cuda-nvidia-version;_linux.run \ --target cuda_&cuda-version;_&cuda-nvidia-version;_linux \ --noexec && pushd cuda_&cuda-version;_&cuda-nvidia-version;_linux/builds && rm -rvf bin integration NVIDIA*.run && mv version.json /opt/cuda-&cuda-version; && mkdir -p /opt/cuda-&cuda-version;/bin && cuda_components=( cuda_nsight/{bin,nsightee_plugins} cuda_sanitizer_api/compute-sanitizer nsight_compute nsight_systems ) for i in ${cuda_components[@]}; do cp -vR $i /opt/cuda-&cuda-version; done && rm -rf cuda_nsight cuda_sanitizer_api && ln -sfv ../compute-sanitizer/compute-sanitizer \ /opt/cuda-&cuda-version;/bin/compute-sanitizer && mv EULA.txt /opt/cuda-&cuda-version; && for lib in *; do cp -vR $lib/* /opt/cuda-&cuda-version; && rm -rf $lib done && ln -svf lib64 /opt/cuda-&cuda-version;/lib && for mf in $(find /opt/cuda-&cuda-version; -name Makefile); do sed -i "s|/usr/local/cuda|/opt/cuda-&cuda-version;|g" "$mf" done && popd && rm -rf cuda_&cuda-version;_&cuda-nvidia-version;_linux

note.para: The above instructions extract the runfile and installs components manually. In the past, this was not needed. However, cuda_installer fails to load as it relies on an older libxml2 ABI. This process will be kept even when the ABI gets fixed as it allows for more control, despite being more complicated.

sect2.para: Still as the &root; user, allow the use of new compilers:

important.para: This technically is not supported and there may be compilation errors in other packages as a result. However, it's a better compromise than installing older compilers just for one package. This package is still being developed but takes a long time to adapt to newer software.

sect2.title: Command Explanations
sect2.para: --target: This parameter specifies the extraction directory.
--noexec: This parameter does ensures the toolkit does not get triggered for installation.

sect2.title: Configuring CUDA
sect2.para: Ensure the libraries are cached as the &root; user:
Now in order to use the toolkit, it needs to be included in the path.
As the &root; user, create the profile (dependent on The Bash Shell Startup Files) for CUDA:

screen.userinput:
cat > /etc/profile.d/cuda.sh << "EOF" # Begin /etc/profile.d/cuda.sh
pathprepend /opt/cuda/bin PATH
# End /etc/profile.d/cuda.sh EOF

sect2.para: Immediately after installation, update the current PATH for your current shell:

sect2.title: Contents
sect2.para: For a full package listing, check Arch's CUDA package contents as the full list is too expansive to list here.
